name: RabbitMQ — single node (calibration #6, RabbitMQ 4.0 classic queue)

descriptions.bench: A publisher driving one RabbitMQ classic queue directly, swept toward saturation. The vendor run used AMQP 0.9.1 with 12-byte messages, persistent + 192 publisher confirms, QoS prefetch 200. (The client speaks HTTP in the catalog; here it stands for the AMQP-speaking benchmark client — the numeric capacity path is protocol-agnostic.)
descriptions.rabbit: One RabbitMQ classic queue modeled as a fixed-throughput store: the `throughput` cell (msg/s) is the single write path's ceiling that sets rho — there is no concurrency/pool knob (a broker's per-queue write is the bottleneck). throughput is the fitted tunable; its catalog default (20,000 msg/s) is an ESTIMATE corrected to the measured 88,534 msg/s.
